symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse crampy abdominal pain with frequent watery diarrhoea, recurrent flare pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent, stereotyped, self-limiting crampy diarrhoeal flares following gastroenteritis are the defining pattern of post-infectious IBS with visceral hypersensitivity.
Watery diarrhoea and cramps are non-specific and shared with infective causes; the recurrence pattern favours the functional diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope / lightheadedness on standing with fatigue and dry mouth

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic dizziness, fatigue and dry mucosae in the setting of high-volume diarrhoeal loss indicate mild hypovolaemia — the driver of safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cramps provoked by oral intake and vomiting of an oral tablet — poor oral tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gastrocolic-reflex exaggeration and visceral hypersensitivity produce pain on ingestion in functional flares.
Poor oral tolerance limits rehydration and is the key factor that could convert a community-managed flare into one needing IV fluids.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis, no melaena, no fresh blood per rectum; continuing to pass stool and flatus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any blood in stool or vomit and no black stool makes a significant GI bleed unlikely.
Continued passage of stool and flatus argues strongly against complete obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no sick contacts, episode identical to prior non-infectious flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of fever and sick contacts, with a stereotyped recurrence, reduces the probability of an acute infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse non-focal abdominal tenderness without RLQ localisation, rebound or Rovsing's sign

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal RLQ pain, rebound and Rovsing's sign on the in-person examination argues against appendicitis — a sealed clinician finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain and must instead safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, distended, non-rigid abdomen with hyperactive bowel sounds in all quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft non-rigid abdomen with hyperactive (not absent/tinkling) bowel sounds argues against mechanical obstruction and peritonitis — sealed clinician examination findings.
Soft abdomen with hyperactive sounds fits functional hypermotility rather than structural pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild postural tachycardia (HR 108) with normotension (BP 105/70), afebrile, SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia with preserved blood pressure indicates mild volume depletion, not shock — clinician-measured vitals sealed from the AI Doctor, who must reason from the patient's reported dizziness and a wearable heart-rate reading.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a stereotyped recurrent diarrhoeal flare with a comprehensively NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen (no GI bleeding, non-focal soft non-rigid abdomen, passing stool/flatus, afebrile) is what licenses confident community management. No single element is diagnostic; the gestalt of a benign pattern plus a clean danger-screen is.

Presyncope, poor oral tolerance and mild postural tachycardia together define a mild dehydration that is community-manageable now but is the axis along which this case could deteriorate — hence the mandatory rehydration advice and escalation triggers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical reassessment of hydration and oral tolerance (GP or telehealth review within 24 hours)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The key variable is whether oral rehydration succeeds. Prompt review lets a clinician reassess hydration, postural vitals and oral tolerance, and arrange IV fluids if the patient cannot keep fluids down.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool testing (MC&S, and faecal calprotectin) at GP if flares persist or alarm features emerge
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not needed acutely; supports longitudinal work-up to exclude infective and inflammatory causes and to characterise the post-infectious picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (FBC, U&E, CRP) if dehydration progresses or the patient attends in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would quantify renal function and volume status and screen for an inflammatory/infective component if the flare does not settle — an in-person/ED investigation, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal imaging (CT / erect chest and abdominal X-ray)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a benign functional flare with a negative acute abdominal screen. Ordering acute imaging here reflects over-investigation driven by the alarming framing rather than the clinical findings — reserved for cases where a surgical abdomen is genuinely suspected.
